batch_summary: Ross anxiously waits in a hospital waiting room with Monica, Chandler, Rachel, and Joey for Carol to arrive and give birth. The group exchanges jokes to ease the tension, and Phoebe arrives with her guitar. When Carol and Susan finally arrive, Ross is frustrated that they stopped for a candy bar. The scene shifts to the delivery room, where Ross and Susan continue to bicker until Dr. Franzblau arrives to check on Carol, diffusing the tension with lighthearted humor.
cumulative_summary: In a hospital waiting room, an anxious Ross paces as he waits for his pregnant ex-wife, Carol, to arrive and give birth to his baby. His friends Monica, Chandler, Rachel, and Joey try to keep him calm, though their teasing and jokes only highlight his nervousness. Phoebe soon joins them, carrying her guitar to pass the time. Tension rises when Carol finally arrives in a wheelchair, accompanied by her partner Susan; Ross is immediately annoyed to learn they delayed their arrival to buy a "Chunky" candy bar at the gift shop. The group moves to the hospital room, where Ross and Susan continue their petty bickering over the candy bar. The mood lightens slightly when Dr. Franzblau enters the room, greeting the parenting team and making playful jokes about Carol's advanced stage of pregnancy as he prepares to examine her.

batch_summary: Inside the hospital room, Ross and Susan continue to bicker, this time over timing Carol's contractions. When Carol asks for ice chips, Ross leaves to get them, but Rachel beats him to it. Rachel introduces herself to Dr. Franzblau in a humorously convoluted way. Meanwhile, in the waiting room, Monica admits she wants a baby, prompting a sarcastic response from Chandler. Nearby, Joey watches a basketball game and strikes up a conversation with Lydia, a pregnant Knicks fan who just walked in.

batch_summary: Joey and Lydia's playful basketball rivalry is interrupted when Lydia goes into labor. When Joey frantically calls for the father, Lydia reveals she is on her own, prompting Joey to kindly guide her to the delivery room. Meanwhile, in the waiting room, Phoebe sings a humorously bleak song about parenting until Ross pays her to stop. Monica's baby fever intensifies when she spots a mother with twins, leading her to complain to Chandler about the unfairness of her situation.

batch_summary: Chandler tries to comfort Monica by suggesting a pact to have a baby together if they are both single at 40, but Monica takes offense, forcing Chandler to awkwardly flee. batch_summary: Joey speaks briefly with Lydia's mother on the phone before she hangs up. When Joey asks about the baby's father, Lydia gets defensive, prompting Joey to briefly walk out.
